article_title: GSI Experiment Finds First Evidence of an Eta-Prime Mesic Nucleus, Probing the Origin of Mass
summary: Substantively excellent η′-mesic nucleus piece — all phys.org-attributed claims (despite phys.org being 403'd) are independently verifiable in ScienceDaily and EurekAlert! — but one order-of-magnitude error: 'less than one millionth of a second' should be 'ten-millionth' per ScienceDaily.

findings.message: Order-of-magnitude error: 'bound configuration persists for less than one millionth of a second'
findings.details: ScienceDaily actually says 'less than ten-millionth of a second' (10⁻⁷ s, not 10⁻⁶ s). Furthermore, ScienceDaily refers to mesons generally, not specifically the bound configuration. EurekAlert! says only 'short-lived bound quantum state' without a numeric lifetime. Correct to 'ten-millionth' or rephrase to match ScienceDaily's actual scope.

content_preview.title: GSI Experiment Finds First Evidence of an Eta-Prime Mesic Nucleus, Probing the Origin of Mass
content_preview.summary: An international collaboration at GSI/FAIR in Germany reports the first experimental evidence that an eta-prime meson can be bound inside a carbon-11 nucleus, with data hinting that the meson loses mass in nuclear matter.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

An international collaboration working at the GSI Helmholtzzentrum fuer Schwerionenforschung in Darmstadt, Germany, has reported the first experimental evidence for an exotic atomic-nucleus state in which an eta-prime meson is bound to a carbon-11 nucleus exclusively through the strong nuclear force. The result, published in Physical Review Letters, addresses a long-predicted but never-observed object — an eta-prime mesic nucleus — and the data also indicate that the meson's mass ap...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
Correct 'one millionth of a second' to 'ten-millionth' to match ScienceDaily.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Science & Research News piece. Technically rigorous, hedged correctly on 'evidence for' vs 'observation', good explanation of the QCD/mass-generation context.
editor_notes.source_verification: Phys.org 403'd but all 5 phys.org-attributed claims are independently verifiable in ScienceDaily (source-1) and/or EurekAlert! (source-2): GSI/FAIR + FRS + WASA setup; proton-on-12C → 11C + forward deuteron knockout; paper title/PRL citation/R. Sekiya authorship; Itahashi (Osaka) as senior author/proposer; in-medium η′ mass shift. EurekAlert claims (Giessen team Metag/Scheidenberger, ~99% strong-interaction mass) verified. ScienceDaily claims (excitation spectrum patterns, 'evidence for', mass change 'may' indicate, no public sigma, follow-up planned) verified. Single error: 'one millionth' should be 'ten-millionth' (order of magnitude).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All material physics is correct except one order-of-magnitude error on the meson lifetime. Single fix.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, scientific. Hedging language matches the source's caution.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Distinct from yesterday's Curiosity / Mars bathtub ring science coverage.
concerns: Single order-of-magnitude error on lifetime; otherwise rigorous.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent particle/nuclear physics piece. Single number fix and ready for re-review.
